title: Festive Fermented Uruguayan Jelly Bake
description: A twist on traditional Uruguayan desserts, this jelly bake is made with fermented ingredients and a katsuramuki-cut crust. Perfect for the holiday season!

ingredients.name: Egg replacer
ingredients.notes: e.g. flaxseed meal mixed with water

instructions: Combine rice flour, potassium sorbate, and coconut sugar in a large mixing bowl.
Add the katsuramuki-cut yam to the bowl and mix until well combined.
In a separate bowl, whisk together water, active dry yeast, and chilli powder.
Pour the yeast mixture over the yam mixture and mix until a smooth batter forms.
Add the egg replacer to the batter and mix until fully incorporated.
Transfer the batter to a greased 8-inch round cake pan or jelly mold.
Cover the pan with plastic wrap and let ferment at room temperature (about 75°F) for 2-3 hours.
Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C) and bake the jelly for 30 minutes, or until set.
Remove the jelly from the oven and let cool on a wire rack for at least 30 minutes.
Once cooled, transfer the jelly to a serving plate and garnish with chopped fresh herbs, if desired.
